name: London Heathrow
notes: Major refinery pipeline access via Esso West London terminal. Multi-supplier redundancy.

name: Frankfurt Airport
notes: Rhine pipeline supply. Dependent on Rotterdam refinery output for ~40% of volume.

name: Paris Charles de Gaulle
notes: Trapil pipeline from Le Havre refineries. Strong domestic refining base.

name: Amsterdam Schiphol
notes: Adjacent to Rotterdam/Antwerp refinery complex. Excellent pipeline infrastructure.

name: Madrid Barajas
notes: CLH pipeline from coastal refineries. Summer tourism drives seasonal demand spikes.

name: Barcelona El Prat
notes: Supplied by Tarragona refinery complex. Summer peak can stress capacity.

name: Rome Fiumicino
notes: Limited domestic refining. Relies on Mediterranean imports and Sarroch pipeline.

name: Munich Airport
notes: TAL pipeline from Trieste. Landlocked — dependent on cross-border pipeline supply.

name: Zurich Airport
notes: No domestic refineries. Fully import-dependent via SAPPRO pipeline from Genoa/Marseille.

name: Vienna Airport
notes: Schwechat refinery nearby but limited jet fuel output. AWP pipeline supplement.

name: Istanbul Airport
notes: TUPRAS domestic refining plus Black Sea import terminals. High-volume hub.

name: Dublin Airport
notes: No domestic refineries since Whitegate closure. All fuel imported via tanker.

name: Copenhagen Kastrup
notes: No local refining. Supplied by tanker from Baltic and North Sea refineries.

name: Oslo Gardermoen
notes: Mongstad refinery pipeline but limited capacity. Seasonal demand volatility.

name: Lisbon Humberto Delgado
notes: Sines refinery pipeline access. Growing tourism traffic straining supply margins.

name: Stockholm Arlanda
notes: No nearby refineries. Supplied by tanker via Göteborg and rail from Preem Lysekil refinery.
